records.ety: [AS. tellan, from talu tale, number, speech; akin to D. tellen to count, G. zählen, OHG. zellen to count, tell, say, Icel. telja, Dan. tale to speak, tælle to count. See Tale that which is told.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Told (tōld); p. pr. & vb. n. Telling.]

defs: To mention one by one, or piece by piece; to recount; to enumerate; to reckon; to number; to count; as, to tell money.
quotes: He telleth the number of the stars.
Tell the joints of the body.

defs: To utter or recite in detail; to give an account of; to narrate.
quotes: Of which I shall tell all the array.
And not a man appears to tell their fate.

defs: To make known; to publish; to disclose; to divulge.
quotes: Why didst thou not tell me that she was thy wife?

defs: To give instruction to; to make report to; to acquaint; to teach; to inform.
quotes: A secret pilgrimage, That you to-day promised to tell me of?

defs: To order; to request; to command.
quotes: He told her not to be frightened.

defs: To discern so as to report; to ascertain by observing; to find out; to discover; as, I can not tell where one color ends and the other begins.
To make account of; to regard; to reckon; to value; to estimate.
quotes: I ne told no dainity of her love.
notes: ☞ Tell, though equivalent in some respect to speak and say, has not always the same application. We say, to tell truth or falsehood, to tell a number, to tell the reasons, to tell something or nothing; but we never say, to tell a speech, discourse, or oration, or to tell an argument or a lesson. It is much used in commands; as, tell me the whole story; tell me all you know.

defs: To give an account; to make report.
quotes: That I may publish with the voice of thankgiving, and tell of all thy wondrous works.

defs: To take effect; to produce a marked effect; as, every shot tells; every expression tells.
quotes: Lest they should tell on us, saying, So did David.

defs: That which is told; tale; account.
quotes: I am at the end of my tell.
